title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Content Render Policy
summary: The UI injects feed HTML, opens deceptive links, depends on card color, loses focus after updates, hides source context, and forces infinite scroll. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go content render policy increment in keyboard-first content-safe feed reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Content Render Policy · Theory Feed Go Content Render Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2453 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state.
